anatomy.description: Icon button on the left side. Typically a back arrow or close icon. Tapping navigates back or closes the current screen. Can be hidden or replaced with a custom icon.
Center area of the header. Shows one of: a title (with optional subtitle), a search bar, or a date/month selector with dropdown chevron. Only one mode is active at a time.

subParts.description: Primary label of the current screen. Bold, centered by default. Can be left-aligned when back button is absent.
Optional supporting text shown below the title. Smaller, muted color.
Month/date label with a dropdown chevron. Tapping opens a date/month picker. Used on calendar or plan screens.
Full-width pill input for search. Replaces the title area when active. Has customer and driver visual variants.

anatomy.description: Right section containing 0–2 icon buttons (btn-header) OR a single accent action button (e.g. 'Finish'). Can be hidden entirely.

subParts.description: Secondary icon-only button. Uses the btn-header component. Up to 2 can appear simultaneously.
Accent CTA button with a text label (e.g. 'Finish'). Appears instead of icon buttons when a primary action is needed.

variants.name: back
variants.description: Shows or hides the back/close button on the left side.

variants.name: Title
variants.description: Shows or hides the title text in the center.

variants.name: button
variants.description: Shows an accent action button (e.g. 'Finish') on the right side instead of icon buttons.

variants.name: search
variants.description: Replaces the title with a Search component in the center area.

variants.name: no icon
variants.description: Hides all right-side icon buttons. Use when no right action is needed.

variants.name: description
variants.description: Shows a supporting subtitle below the title.

variants.name: date
variants.description: Replaces the title with a date/month selector with a dropdown chevron.

variants.name: 2nd icon
variants.description: Shows or hides the second icon button in the right actions area.

variantCombinations.description: Default: title only, no back, two icon slots on right.
Back + title + right icons. Most common pattern for secondary screens.
Back only, no title, right icons visible.
Back + search bar. Used when entering search mode.
Back + title + subtitle (description). Used for screens needing context below the title.
Title + accent action button, no back.
Back + accent action button only. No title, no icon buttons.
Date/month selector with dropdown, right icons, no back.
Back + date/month selector with dropdown + right icons. Used on calendar/plan screens reached via navigation.
screenExamples: User: Plan screen with back navigation
Rides date range view with back

variantCombinations.description: Back + title only. No right actions.
Title only, centered. No back, no right actions.

sizeNote: Single size. Full screen width (390px reference). Height is determined by content — typically 56–64px including safe area padding.

subComponents.name: btn-header
subComponents.description: Icon-only button used for left and right header actions. Has default and pressed states. Accepts any icon via INSTANCE_SWAP.

subComponents.name: Search
subComponents.description: Search input component used in header search mode. Has customer and driver visual types. States: Default, focused, writing.

usageContext: Used as the top navigation bar on all screens except the Home screen (which uses header-home).
Positioned at the top of the screen below the system status bar.
Safe area inset (paddingTop) must be applied to account for device notches and status bars.
Used across both customer app (ds-customer) and driver app (ds-drivers).
notes: Variant axis naming in Figma is inconsistent (back vs Title vs no icon) — treat all as boolean toggles.
The search mode (search=on) uses the Search sub-component, which has customer and driver variants.
The date selector mode (date=on) shows a month/year label with a dropdown chevron — it does not embed a full picker inline.
This is a core component. App-specific extended variants (e.g. header-plan, header-rides) are defined in ds-customer and ds-drivers respectively.
Do not confuse with header-home (ds-customer), which is exclusive to the Home screen and has a completely different structure (event pill, avatar, chat button).
